title: CI-RED-016-A: manifest-discipline bleed-stop wired into Core CI
intent: CI-RED-016-A is the small enforcement slice of CI-RED-016: stop net-new router files from bypassing router_registry discipline while the larger endpoint consolidation stays deferred. It is a ratchet, not a cleanup migration - it does not manifest the 107 grandfathered routers, resolve endpoint ownership, reorder precedence, change live endpoint winners, or close CI-RED-016. Tighten the committed baseline from 108 to 107 (remove the one healed entry routers/cam/geometry_authority_router, now manifested by CI-RED-015-H), add focused unit tests for check_manifest_discipline.py, wire the check into Core CI before API boot/full pytest, and correct stale UNRUN/uncommitted status language.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) manifest_discipline_baseline.txt: tightened 108->107, removing only routers/cam/geometry_authority_router, which healed when CI-RED-015-H manifested it (RouterSpec module=app.routers.cam.geometry_authority_router in cam_manifest.py). Witnessed by set difference (comm -3 of header-stripped sorted sets), not line diff: exactly one healed entry, zero net-new. (2) check_manifest_discipline.py: replaced the 'UNRUN as of 2026-05-30' docstring STATUS block with materialized/baseline-backed/CI-wired status; ratchet semantics (fail only on net-new unmanifested routers) unchanged, no logic edit. (3) tests/test_manifest_discipline.py (new): 12 focused unit tests importing the script by path (no app.main import) with tmp_path miniature app tree and monkeypatched APP_ROOT/MANIFEST_DIR/BASELINE_PATH; covers module_to_relpath, decorator detection, non-router ignore, manifested/aggregator exclusion, net-new fail (exit 1), healed-reported-pass, --update-baseline sorted rewrite, clean pass, and missing-baseline bootstrap; negative fixtures live only in tmp_path. (4) core_ci.yml: added a 'Manifest discipline bleed-stop' step to the api-tests job after 'Cost Attribution Unit Tests' and before 'Boot API'/'Run pytest'. (5) CI_RED_015D_OPEN_DECISIONS.md: annotated stale uncommitted/untracked/unrun language as historical and added a CI-RED-016-A status note; 015-D closure logic and CI-RED-016 deferral preserved. (6) SPRINTS.md: recorded CI-RED-016-A subnote under CI-RED-016 as a bleed-stop only. HARDENING FOLLOW-UP (review response — supersedes 'no logic change' / '12 tests' above where they differ): (a) DETECTION BROADENED — _ROUTER_DECORATOR now also matches websocket|head|options|trace|api_route, so a websocket/api_route-only router file can no longer slip past the ratchet (previously only get|post|put|patch|delete). Verified this is pure future-proofing: the current tree's only websocket router (app/websocket/router.py) already carried an @router.get, so the detected set is byte-identical — baseline stays 107, zero grandfathering. (b) FAIL-HARD IN CI — added --require-baseline: a missing baseline now exits 2 instead of silently re-bootstrapping+passing (which would mask the check on a bad checkout/path); the Core CI step now passes --require-baseline. Local bootstrap behaviour is unchanged when the flag is absent. (c) removed a vacuous no-op __init__.py branch in find_router_files (cleanup, behaviour identical). (d) tests grew 12->18: broadened-verb detection, --require-baseline hard-fail + normal-ratchet, and three REPRESENTATIVE real-tree guards (checker output == committed baseline; geometry_authority_router is genuinely covered not prematurely dropped; KNOWN_AGGREGATOR_PKGS pinned so the prefix-allowlist trust boundary can't silently grow). Documented remaining accepted limitations in the docstring: the aggregator prefix is an allow-list not a composition proof, and programmatic add_api_route/app.websocket registration is not detected.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: No prior workflow invoked check_manifest_discipline.py, so the ratchet was inert - a net-new unmanifested @router file could land silently. This PR makes it a CI witness step and tightens the baseline to the current healed count so the grandfathered set cannot silently grow. The test file did not exist, so the checker had zero coverage. Endpoint consolidation (CI-RED-016) is untouched and remains open.

commands_run: cd services/api && python scripts/check_manifest_discipline.py -> OK: no net-new unmanifested router files (baseline holds 107)
cd services/api && python scripts/check_manifest_discipline.py --require-baseline -> OK (baseline present)
set witness: comm -3 <(before) <(after) -> only left-only routers/cam/geometry_authority_router; right-only empty; before=108 after=107

review-hardening broadened-regex witness: comm -3 of baseline before/after --update-baseline with the widened verb set -> EMPTY both sides (107==107, byte-identical); app/websocket/router.py already carried @router.get so no new grandfathering

cd services/api && python -m pytest tests/test_manifest_discipline.py -q -> 18 passed (api venv), incl. real-tree checker==baseline, geometry_authority_router covered, aggregator-pkgs pinned, --require-baseline hard-fail
yaml.safe_load(core_ci.yml) parse OK; 'Manifest discipline bleed-stop' step now runs --require-baseline before 'Boot API'

files.intent: Tighten baseline 108->107; remove only the healed geometry_authority_router entry
files.behavior_change: none - frozen-set ratchet data, tightened after a witnessed heal

files.intent: Correct stale UNRUN status; broaden decorator detection (websocket/api_route/head/options/trace); add --require-baseline hard-fail; drop vacuous __init__ branch
files.behavior_change: wider detection (byte-identical set on current tree) + CI-only fail-hard on missing baseline; ratchet semantics otherwise unchanged

files.intent: 18 unit tests (12 miniature tmp_path + 6 review-response: broadened verbs, --require-baseline, and real-tree checker==baseline / geometry_authority_router-covered / aggregator-pkgs-pinned)
Run the manifest-discipline check with --require-baseline in api-tests before API boot/full pytest
Annotate stale uncommitted/untracked/unrun language as historical; preserve 015-D closure logic
Record CI-RED-016-A as a bleed-stop only under CI-RED-016
